{"description":"Research: Optuna integration points across Gas Town rigs

CONTEXT — STARTUP FACTORY (from Mayor):

We have 1000 startup ideas going into an automated pipeline. 100 become real startups in a year. Every startup is a Gas Town formula — composable, built from formulas we develop in sf_workflows. The pipeline itself is formula-driven: idea → triage → standup → tuning → community → tokenization. Nothing manual.

Petals gives us decentralized inference — a swarm that grows as community GPU providers join. Node0 and LoRA Forge handle fine-tuning — custom LoRA adapters per agent, per startup. Every startup runs on this shared compute layer. Community members provide GPUs as stakeholders. More towns = more GPUs = more capacity = more startups. Training data feeds back into better LoRA adapters. Better agents attract more community. More community brings more compute. The infrastructure and startups grow each other.

Each startup gets tokenized — ownership tracked through the full lifecycle. GPU providers earn through the swarm. Community contributors earn through the startups they help build. Token holders govern the direction. Dolt federation enables multiple Gas Towns syncing state, sharing infrastructure, running startups across machines.

Every layer feeds every other layer:
- Startups run on decentralized compute → compute grows from community → community grows from startups
- Agents generate training data → training makes better agents → better agents attract community
- Formulas build startups → startups prove formulas → proven formulas build more startups
- Tokens align incentives → aligned incentives grow community → community powers everything

RESEARCH SCOPE:

1. Survey all rigs that involve tunable parameters: lora_forge (LoRA training hyperparams), node0 (model selection, adapter config), petals (inference routing/scheduling), sf_workflows (formula parameter tuning), pi_agent_rust (agent behavior tuning), hivemind (swarm coordination params)
2. For each rig, identify: what parameters exist today, how they are currently set, and where Optuna could automate optimization
3. Consider how Optuna fits the factory flywheel: automated HPO for startup-specific LoRA adapters, agent behavior tuning per role, inference routing optimization, formula parameter search
4. Produce a research summary in crew-docs/optuna-integration-map.md listing each rig, its integration surface, estimated effort, and priority

OUTPUT: crew-docs/optuna-integration-map.md with per-rig analysis
This bead will spawn follow-up epics in each relevant rig once the map is complete."}

3. Produce a research summary in crew-docs/optuna-integration-map.md listing each rig, its integration surface, estimated effort, and priority
